summary:
Acrocephaly refers to an abnormal prominence of the frontal bone causing a high or domed head shape; it encompasses various forms including oxycephaly among other conditions related to craniostenosis. Thus, acrocephaly can be classified broadly along with similar congenital malformations making 'All of the Above' correct. \boxed{D}

The summary captures that acrocephaly involves a distinctive front part of the skull while acknowledging its relation through classification schemes where multiple terms describe variations thereof fitting into group D ('all of the above'). The answer choice reflects these interconnected diagnoses effectively. ``` Summary
Acrocephaly denotes a condition marked by a prominently rounded top of the head due to premature closure of the sutures at the crown, often categorized similarly to other craniosynostoses such as oxycephaly. This makes categorizing acrocephaly under all described options appropriate. 
```
